name: Fresh Berry Tulip W/White Chocolate "Ice Cream
recipeIngredient: 1 pint Blueberries, stems removed and washed
recipeInstructions:
EQUIPMENT: Measuring cup, measuring spoons, paring knife, 9-inch cake circle, parchment paper, electric mixer with paddle and balloon whisk, rubber spatula, double boiler, whisk, 2 baking sheets, cake spatula, 46-ounce juice can, 100%-cotton kitchen towel, food processor with metal blade, ice cream scoop
1. With a pencil, trace a 9-inch circle on each of 8 sheets of parchment paper (each one cut to fit a baking sheet). Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with a paddle, combine butter and 1 1/2 cups sugar. Beat the butter on low for 1 minute, on medium for 1 minute, and on high for 1 minute. Scrape down the sides of the bowl and beat on high for 1 additional minute.
3. Heat 1 inch of water in the bottom half of a double boiler over high heat. Place egg whites in the top half of the double boiler and continuously whisk the whites while heating to a temperature of 110°F, about 1 minute.
4. Slowly pour 1/2 of the beaten whites into the butter mixture while mixing on high, about 1 minute. Scrape down the bowl. Slowly add the remaining whites while mixing on high. Scrape down the bowl. Mix on high for 15 more seconds.
5. Add vanilla extract and beat on high for 5 seconds. Add flour, mixing on low for 10 to 15 seconds, until the batter pulls away from the sides of the bowl. Remove the bowl from the mixer.
6. Use a rubber spatula to finish mixing the batter, until it is smooth and thoroughly combined.
